title: feat: update Gemini Flash Lite to 3.5
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Updates the general Flash-Lite service and gemini-search-fast from Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite to stable Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite.
- Preserves every existing 3.1 and generic alias, adds 3.5 aliases, and keeps tools, native web search, structured output, reasoning, streaming, and text/image/audio/video input.
- Pins OpenRouter to google/gemini-3.5-flash-lite on google-vertex/global with fallbacks disabled.
- Updates posted rates to $0.30/M input, $0.03/M cache read, and $2.50/M output; search and cache-storage billing rules remain unchanged.
- Verified with both worker typechecks, 442 focused tests, direct OpenRouter probes, and authenticated local E2E across aliases, modalities, search tool shapes, cache, paid-only access, errors, usage headers, and burst traffic. The max_tokens=1 usage omission is identical on the old and new upstream routes and remains tracked in the separate pricing work.

gist.headline: Flash Lite got fresher
gist.blurb: The fast little Gemini lane now runs on stable Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite. Same familiar aliases, more current nectar under the hood.
gist.summary: Updated the general Flash-Lite service and gemini-search-fast from Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite to stable Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, retaining all existing aliases, multimodal inputs, tools, native web search, structured output, reasoning, and streaming.
gist.impact: Users can keep using their existing Flash-Lite and Gemini search aliases while requests now route to Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite at $0.30/M input, $0.03/M cache reads, and $2.50/M output.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy model-upgrade garden: Polli tends a flowering retro terminal labeled “Flash Lite 3.5,” while glowing prompt seeds become text, image, audio, and video sprouts. The scene represents a familiar API path receiving a newer Gemini engine without losing its tools, search, streaming, or aliases.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow bee with dark brown stripes, black antennae, oval eyes, bright pink rectangular cheek blushes, a happy smile, rounded translucent light-blue wings, and tiny mechanic goggles, carefully installing a glowing “3.5” flower cartridge into a retro terminal surrounded by code-plants that bloom into small text, image, audio, and video icons. Prominent soft lime green (#ecf874) terminal glow and pastel mint-lavender-peach garden, with simple pixel-font text “FLASH LITE 3.5”; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.

Prominent soft lime green (#ecf874) terminal glow and pastel mint-lavender-peach garden, with simple pixel-font text “FLASH LITE 3.5”; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
